about.title: Identification Card
about.specialtyValue: Fullstack development, mobile apps, edge computing
about.availabilityValue: Looking for my first formal role
about.bio: My programming journey has been 100% self-taught. My curiosity to understand how things work has led me to explore everything from mobile development to distributed systems design. I don't have a traditional background, but I make up for it by building real projects, studying complex architectures, and writing code I can be proud of. I am currently looking for my first full-time professional role to add value and keep growing.

capabilities.edgeValue: Cloudflare Workers, Durable Objects, Firebase FCM
capabilities.architectureValue: Plugin-first, Layered, Repository Pattern, LWW CRDT

philosophy: Learn by building: every expedient is born from a real need — Kumoriya from content fragmentation, MyBookTrace from my own reading habit, POS from B2B needs, Matrixezz from a linear algebra class.

Architecture serves the problem, not the other way around: plugin-first when the domain changes frequently (Kumoriya), layered architecture when separation of concerns matters (Finwise, MyBookTrace, POS).
Local-first when offline matters: MyBookTrace with sqflite, Kumoriya with Drift + CRDT. The network is an enhancement, not a dependency.
Document the why, not just the what: Kumoriya's ADRs and dev-diary are the example. Code explains how; documentation captures decisions and dead ends.
about.note: Each tool's context is detailed in the expedients. Only production-verified capabilities are listed here.
